column4: Åbo Akademi University is Finland’s only Swedish-language multidisciplinary university, founded in 1918, with its main activities in two campuses: Åbo (Turku) and Vasa. It has around 7,000 students, including about 1,000 international students each year. The official language of instruction is Swedish, but there is a wide array of courses taught in English (around 250 annually) for exchange students. Turku (Åbo in Swedish) is Finland’s oldest city and a charming place to live and study. With over 40,000 students in the city and a historic, cultural atmosphere, there's always something going on — theatre, museums or festivals. Turku lies on the southwest coast of Finland, close to the archipelago, has good transport links to other parts of Finland and beyond, and provides a picturesque natural environment (coastline, islands, parks) for both study and recreation.

column4: The university was founded in 1885 and is among the largest universities in the US with more than 74,000 students from 120 countries. ASU has four campuses located throughout the Phoenix metropolitan area, but are easily accessible by free intercampus shuttles. While international exchange students can study at any campus, most take courses at the Tempe campus due to the high number of classes and programs offered. Tempe is a lively university city, only a five-minute drive from Phoenix.

column18: Course Restrictions: W.P. Carey School of Business (undergraduate programs): seats are limited depending on the specific academic unit or course; one example is Supply Chain Management (SCM) which has extremely limited spaces for exchange students. W.P. Carey School of Business (MBA): prior approval required during exchange program nomination phase (limited course offerings). Herberger Institute for Design and the Arts: students studying a similar major at their home university will be required to submit a portfolio of work or audition to gain access to courses. Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering: Engineering students cannot take any engineering capstone courses at ASU. In addition, some schools in engineering have further enrollment restrictions for engineering students: Schools of Computing and Augmented Intelligence: Students can take any lower division courses they wish. For upper division courses, they need instructor approval to receive access to the course. Sustainable Engineering and the Built Environment: If there are a large number of open seats, students will be allowed to enroll in courses for which they meet prerequisites. If there are very few seats available, advisors will wait until 1-2 weeks before the start of the semester before giving permission for exchange students to enroll. Engineering of Matter, Transport, and Energy: After all registration has opened, students may be given overrides into courses that still have a lot of seats open, depending on the course demand for ASU majors. For these courses, advisers will wait until the FIRST WEEK of the semester before giving permission for exchange students to enroll. Walter Cronkite School of Journalism & Mass Comm: JMC courses are not available; MCO courses are limited. Alternative courses in other colleges may be found under the following prefixes: COM & ENG. College of Health Solutions: seats are limited depending on the specific academic unit or course. Edson College of Nursing and Health Innovation: most courses are restricted. Mary Lou Fulton Teacher’s College: most courses are restricted.

column21: Students should use the most recent semester Class Search (LINK) to search course offerings, as the Course Catalog Search will include many courses that are not currently scheduled. column22: Students have 3 housing options: 1) On-Campus: ASU Residence Halls: On-campus housing is EXTREMELY limited, and is assigned on a first-come, first-served basis! column4: Founded in 1946, the Australian National University (ANU) supports a community of over 25,500 students. The ANU campus is located in Canberra, the capital of Australia and comprises seven academic colleges, which host a number of schools and research centres specialising in a variety of disciplines. The university is especially renowned for the strength of its Arts and Humanities and Natural Sciences departments. ANU courses are taught in small classes and state-of-the-art collaborative spaces that stimulate students to think differently. As ANU students, incoming exchange participants will have the support of our vibrant and talented community including access to academic, sporting, cultural and social activities, and access to comprehensive wellbeing support.  ANU is located in the heart of Canberra, our beautiful and vibrant capital city. Canberra’s accessibility, friendliness, and overall quality of life makes it the perfect place to live, study and explore the region.  Get a glimpse of a day in the life of an ANU exchange student!

column4: Established in 1348, Charles University stands as one of the oldest and most esteemed universities in the world. Despite its rich historical legacy, the university maintains a forward-thinking and cosmopolitan approach, making it a modern and prestigious institution of higher education. As the largest and top-rated university in the Czech Republic, Charles University boasts 17 faculties, with 14 of them located in the vibrant city of Prague. The university's commitment to internationalization is evident in its diverse student body, with over 9,000 international students comprising approximately 20% of the total student population. This multicultural environment fosters a lively and dynamic atmosphere within the university. Offering a wide range of courses in multiple languages, including English, German, French, and Czech, Charles University ensures that students from various backgrounds can pursue their academic goals. As an Erasmus student, one can benefit from the support of international associations dedicated to facilitating the integration and orientation process. Additionally, each faculty hosts various clubs and organizations, such as film clubs, choirs, and book clubs, providing students with opportunities to engage in extracurricular activities and explore their diverse interests. Charles University serves as a thriving hub of intellectual growth, cultural exchange, and community engagement, making it a highly sought-after institution for students seeking a comprehensive and enriching educational experience. Prague offers a rich and stimulating environment for student life, combining historical depth with modern infrastructure. The city is compact and well-connected by public transport, including a metro system known for its depth and efficiency. Students can explore medieval streets, vibrant cultural venues, and green spaces throughout the city. A fun fact: Prague is home to the world’s largest castle complex (the Prague Castle) which spans over 70,000 square meters and includes palaces, churches, and historic lanes, making it a central part of the city’s identity and a frequent destination for students and visitors alike.

column4:
The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK) is a leading comprehensive research university. It has a total of 61 departments under the Faculties of Arts, Business Administration, Education, Engineering, Law, Medicine, Science, and Social Science. Currently, it has approximately 21,000 students undertaking undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral programmes.
CUHK is the only university in Hong Kong with a college system. Each undergraduate student is associated with one of the nine Colleges. The Colleges are responsible for informal learning, and for academic and cultural immersion for students. CUHK is committed to general education and whole-person development.

- College General Education (GECC, GEJC, GEMC, GESH, GEUC, GESC) courses

column4: Cornell University is the federal land-grant institution of New York State, a private endowed university, a member of the Ivy League, and a partner of the State University of New York. It has been described as the first truly American university because of its founders' revolutionarily egalitarian and practical vision of higher education, and is dedicated to its land-grant mission of outreach and public service. Founded in 1865 by Ezra Cornell and Andrew Dickson White, Cornell was intended to teach and make contributions in all fields of knowledge. These ideals are captured in Cornell's motto, a popular 1865 Ezra Cornell quotation: "I would founded an institution where any person can find instruction in any study."

column68: Exchange students accepted at Cornell will be admitted into a major in the college or unit participating in the exchange. It is expected that students applying to the exchange program will be interested in taking the majority of their classes in this college or unit. Enrollment in classes in other colleges at Cornell is possible but cannot be guaranteed. Only students in business degree programs can apply to the Dyson School of Applied Economics and Management or the Nolan School of Hotel Administration as their Home College. Exchange students are unable to take courses in Computer Science (CS). Related subjects may be found in other departments (Electrical and Computer Engineering; Information Science, Systems, and Technology; or Operations Research and Information Engineering). • Studio classes (architecture, art, design, and fashion) are typically not available to students through general exchange agreements. • Senior capstone courses as well as first year writing seminars are not available to exchange students. Graduate programs, and Cornell University degree/certificate programs outside Ithaca, NY are not open to university-wide exchanges.

column4: Established in 1855 as the Federal Polytechnical School, ETH Zurich has from the outset been a national centre for education with international appeal, attracting talent from all over the world. Today, ETH Zurich is one of the leading international universities for technology and the natural sciences. It is well known for its excellent education, ground-breaking fundamental research and for implementing its results directly into practice. ETH Zurich has more than 18,500 students from over 110 countries. 21 Nobel Laureates have studied, taught or conducted research at ETH Zurich, underlining the excellent reputation of the university.
column12: Fall semester: September - February; Spring semester: February - September
column15: CEFR certificate: German/English B2 (accepted). C1 (recommended)
column18: You should have completed two years of study (120 ECTS) before enrolling at ETH. Please note that some courses are restricted, e.g., in the number of participants and that you cannot register for any courses ending with –AAL. 2/3 of your course credits must be offered by the department you are applying to. 1/3 of your credits can be taken from other departments and / or the University of Zurich. Students are free to combine Bachelor and Master courses, independent of their level of studies at the home university, provided they have the necessary prerequisites to follow the courses. There is not limit as to the amount of MSc courses Bachelor students can take. You will be studying at the Department of Mathematics (D-MATH). At D-MATH, the 2/3 rule is counted with respect to courses being part of the BSc/MSc programmes in (Applied) Mathematics or Statistics, not with respect to courses held by the department (401-XXXX-XX). Please note that most of the advanced courses in probability theory, statistics and similar areas require familiarity with measure theory approaches of probability theory. Students who wish to attend these courses must possess solid knowledge of measure theory, for example by having already attended the corresponding courses or by acquiring the knowledge themselves, preferably with the help of a faculty member from the home university. If you want to do a research project or write you bachelor’s thesis at ETH, you must enclose written confirmation from your supervisor at ETH Zurich in your application. Project/thesis work is expected to be full-time. A combination of course work and a thesis/project must be discussed with the supervisor. It is not possible to write your thesis project as an exchange student at ETH Zurich.

Ewha produces strong students capable of working in all corners of the world through diverse and practical international exchange programs.

column82:
Semester Fulfillment
Undergraduate students must have completed at least one (1) semester before the application stage at Ewha begins (i.e. before March for exchange in the following Fall Semester, and before September for exchange in the following Spring Semester).
Graduate students must have completed at least one (1) semester at graduate-level before the application stage at Ewha begins (i.e. before March for exchange in the following Fall Semester, and before September for exchange in the following Spring Semester). Graduate students must provide official transcript of graduate-level studies (undergraduate level GPA is not considered). This means that graduate students should consider Ewha Womans University for exchange only during the third or fourth semester of their graduate programme.

column4: Fudan consists of 29 schools and departments, with seventy undergraduate disciplines. The University confers bachelor's degrees in seventy academic disciplines, and master's degrees in two hundred and twenty five disciplines (with fifty of them established by the University itself), and doctoral degrees in twenty-four Level I and one hundred and fifty-three Level II academic disciplines (with twenty-nine of them established by the University itself). There are also twenty-five research stations that offer postdoctoral fellowships. Fudan now has an enrollment of 26,792 full-time degree candidates. Another 20,670 are studying at the schools of Continuing Education and Online Education. Besides, the University's population of foreign students is 2,812 today.
column18: Please refer to the relevant exchange program offered by Fudan. General Advanced Program students CAN NOT take Chinese Language courses at International Cultural Exchange School (ICES). Other restrictions include: MBA courses, LLM courses, medical courses, special programs in faculties (e.g. International Cultural Exchange School), undergraduate courses in medicine. Effective Spring 2027, students of Chinese nationality are no longer eligible to participate in the Fudan university-level exchange program.

Chinese proficiency: If you intend to take Chinese-taught courses, you need to be either a native speaker of Chinese or have an HSK level 5 score of 210. HSK score is only necessary if you intend to take Chinese-taught courses. You do NOT need an HSK score if you are a native speaker of Chinese or if you do not intend to take Chinese-taught courses.

column4: Goethe Universität Frankfurt am Main is one of Germany’s leading research universities, known for its strong academic tradition and international orientation. Founded in 1914, the university offers a wide range of disciplines across its 16 faculties, with particular strengths in economics, law, social sciences, and natural sciences. It is home to several prestigious research institutes and maintains close ties with the city’s financial and cultural institutions. With a student population of over 45,000, including a significant number of international students, Goethe University fosters a vibrant and diverse academic community. Its commitment to interdisciplinary research and critical inquiry makes it an attractive destination for students seeking a rigorous and globally connected exchange experience. Situated in Frankfurt am Main, Germany’s financial capital and one of Europe’s most cosmopolitan cities, Goethe University offers students a dynamic urban environment rich in professional and cultural opportunities. Frankfurt is known for its impressive skyline, international institutions, and thriving arts scene. The city combines modern infrastructure with historical charm, offering easy access to museums, theaters, and green spaces along the River Main. Its central location in Europe also makes it an ideal base for travel and exploration. For students looking to combine academic excellence with the experience of living in a global metropolis, Goethe Universität Frankfurt am Main presents an outstanding choice for an exchange semester.

With more than 40,000 students, our community spans five campuses across South East Queensland plus our Digital campus. We’re also proud to be home to a global alumni network of over 200,000 graduates.

column4:
Established in 1386, the University of Heidelberg holds the distinction of being the oldest university in Germany, embodying a legacy of knowledge and intellectual pursuit that spans centuries. With a community of over 31,000 students, including approximately 6,000 international students, it provides a vibrant and inclusive environment for higher education. Nestled by the picturesque lake Neckar, the campus offers a serene and inspiring backdrop for academic pursuits, fostering a conducive atmosphere for learning and personal growth.
Recognized as one of the strongest research universities in Europe, the institution boasts 12 faculties encompassing a diverse range of disciplines. These faculties, such as Biosciences, Chemistry and Earth Sciences, Mathematics and Computer Science, Physics and Astronomy, Philosophy, Modern Languages, Theology, Law, Economics and Social Sciences, Behavioral and Cultural Studies, and Medicine, offer comprehensive academic programs that cater to a wide array of interests and career paths. Through a commitment to rigorous research and innovation, the university cultivates an environment that fuels intellectual curiosity and advances knowledge in various fields.
Heidelberg is a compact and picturesque city located along the Neckar River in southwestern Germany. Known for its Baroque architecture, cobblestone streets, and vibrant cultural life, the city offers a student-friendly environment with efficient public transport and a strong cycling culture. The city’s historic center is home to cafés, bookstores, and cultural venues, all within walking distance of the university. A fun fact: tucked inside Heidelberg Castle is the world’s largest wine barrel, the Heidelberg Tun, built in 1751 with a capacity of over 219,000 liters—though it was only filled three times due to persistent leaks.

column4: Humboldt University of Berlin is a public research university in the central borough of Mitte in Berlin, Germany. It was established by Frederick William III on the initiative of Wilhelm von Humboldt, Johann Gottlieb Fichte and Friedrich Ernst Daniel Schleiermacher as the University of Berlin (Universität zu Berlin) in 1809, and opened in 1810, making it the oldest of Berlin's four universities. HU is divided into nine faculties, including its medical school shared with the Free University of Berlin, has a student enrollment of around 33,000 students, and offers degree programmes in some 189 disciplines from undergraduate to postdoctorate level. Past and present faculty and notable alumni include an impressive 57 Nobel Prize laureates as well as eminent philosophers, sociologists, artists, lawyers, politicians, mathematicians, scientists, and Heads of State; among them are Albert Einstein, Karl Marx, Friedrich Engels, Otto von Bismarck, W. E. B. Du Bois, Angela Davis, Arthur Schopenhauer, Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel, Walter Benjamin, Max Weber, Georg Simmel, Karl Liebknecht, Ernst Cassirer, Heinrich Heine, Max Planck, and the Brothers Grimm.

You are allowed to attend the same subject as you are enrolled in in your home university. However, in general, exchange students are also allowed to take part in classes of other departments, but there is no guarantee of admission. The student would need to confirm the possibility of participation and, if necessary, examination upon arrival.

- Bachelor (first three years of study): You may take courses from two degree programmes at Humboldt (e.g. 1. Biology, 2. Social Sciences)
- Master: You can only take courses from one degree programme. (e.g. 1. Biology)

To do research projects at the department, you need to contact the professor who is responsible of the specific research field and organize supervision yourself.

At some departments at Humboldt University, BSc students are allowed to follow MSc courses provided they have the prerequisites.
Mathematics-Economics students cannot follow courses at the Department of Economics.

column4:
The University of Greenland, known as Ilisimatusarfik, is the leading institution of higher education in Greenland and offers a unique academic experience shaped by Arctic perspectives. It is a small university with around 200 to 300 students. The university offers courses mainly in Greenlandic and Danish, with some courses available in English. The main campus is located in Nuuk.
Nuuk is a small yet dynamic city surrounded by dramatic mountains and fjords, offering a blend of traditional Greenlandic culture and modern living. The city is compact and walkable, with short distances between most areas. The natural surroundings provide endless opportunities for outdoor exploration, including hiking, kayaking, and witnessing the northern lights, making it an inspiring place for students seeking both cultural immersion and adventure.

column4: The Karlstad University is a dynamic and modern institution with around 19 000 students and a wide variety of programmes. Courses are primarily taught in Swedish, but there is a healthy selection of single courses in English, making it a solid option for exchange. The city of Karlstad provides a friendly and manageable setting for student life with a nice walkable city centre. Karlstad campus is a few kilometers outside the city but is well connected through public transit. The broader region offers plenty of natural surroundings: Vänern lake, forests, rivers and outdoor options are all close by.

The total number of students is 37,493, (Undergraduate: 20,254, Graduate: 9,257) and 7,982 on the Sejong Campus (Undergraduate: 7,031, Graduate: 951). Among the Anam Campus students 5.81% are international students. The total number of international students in language, exchange and training programs stands at 2,164.

column4: At Linköping University you’ll find around 44 000 students across its multiple campuses. The institution is well known for its innovative education and interdisciplinary research, and you’ll find a strong selection of programmes and individual courses taught in English. The city of Linköping and its nearby twin city Norrköping offer a student-friendly setting. Beyond the city you’ll have access to Swedish countryside, lakes, forests and peaceful nature reserves that are just a short trip away.

column4: Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München (LMU Munich) is one of Europe’s foremost research universities, with a legacy of academic excellence dating back to its founding in 1472. LMU offers a broad spectrum of disciplines, ranging from the humanities and social sciences to medicine and natural sciences, and is consistently ranked among the top universities in Germany and internationally. Its programs are deeply rooted in cutting-edge research, and students benefit from close interaction with leading scholars and access to world-class facilities. With a strong international profile and a student body of over 50,000, LMU provides a stimulating academic environment that encourages intellectual curiosity and interdisciplinary collaboration. Located in the heart of Munich, LMU offers students the opportunity to study in one of Germany’s most attractive and culturally rich cities. Munich is known for its high quality of life, vibrant arts scene, and proximity to the Alps, making it an ideal setting for both academic and personal growth. The city combines historic charm with modern innovation, and students can enjoy everything from museums and music festivals to outdoor activities and international cuisine. An exchange semester at LMU Munich promises not only academic enrichment but also a rewarding experience in one of Europe’s most welcoming and dynamic urban environments.

If you are a bachelor student, you will, as a rule, not be permitted to take master courses (any exceptions to this rule will be discussed and decided by LMU).

LMU Munich has a classical academic profile ranging from the humanities and cultural sciences, law, economics, and social sciences to medicine and natural sciences. The University is divided into 18 faculties and enjoys one of Germany’s finest library systems.

column4: Luleå University of Technology, located in the Arctic Region, has four campuses and over a hundred education programs. The university serves around 18,000 students. It offers programmes and courses in Swedish as the primary language of instruction, but it also has a growing number of courses in English. The city of Luleå sits in Sweden’s far north and provides a distinctive Nordic student experience. The main campus lies within reach of the central city, and everyday necessities are accessible by local transit and cycling when the weather allows. Beyond the urban amenities you’ll find dramatic natural surroundings: forests, coastlines and snow in winter. For an exchange student willing to embrace the climate, Luleå offers both solid academic life and a memorable location.

column4: Lund University is one of Scandinavia’s oldest and largest institutions, with a student population of over 45,000. It is internationally recognized for its research output and participation in major EU projects places it among the leading research universities in northern Europe. Its historical foundation in 1666 and continued emphasis on academic freedom distinguish it from other institutions. The university offers courses taught in both English and Swedish across a wide range of disciplines, making it highly accessible to students from all over the world. The university is situated in the city of Lund in southern Sweden, a compact and walkable city with a strong student presence. Lund combines medieval architecture with modern research parks, and its size makes it easy for students to navigate daily life without relying on public transport.

  Students who wish to take 600-700 level graduate courses at McGill, should apply for graduate admission. It is only possible to apply for graduate admuission for the fall semester so you need to apply in the November application round at UCPH and the application deadline at McGill is very early - often before UCPH is ready with the result of your application(December-February). Student must reach out to the department they are interested in studying with to verify if they will be accepting exchange applications for that term.  Not all graduate departments are open to exchange students and this can vary by term depending on space restrictions.  In general, some programs will never be open and that might include the Health Science programs (Medicine or Nursing, for example) and some, like the MBA program, are only open to students where an exclusive MBA exchange agreement already exists.
  If the program is able to accept the exchange application, they will normally provide the student with application instructions, as they will be using a different web application from undergraduate students.

Please note that the graduate application will have different deadlines and will not follow the dates in the university  Fact Sheet.  Students will need to abide by the graduate application deadlines, which you can find on the programs pages.

Mid Sweden University is an inspiring and dynamic institution with campuses in Ôstersynd and Sundsvall. It has around 14 000 students and two main campuses, one in Sundsvall and one in Östersund. It offers courses taught in Swedish and a number of programmes and single-courses taught in English.
The city settings of the campuses give a great student experience in both Sundsvall and Östersund. You’ll find central neighbourhoods where cafés, shops and student services are within walking distance of campus and accommodation, making day-to-day life convenient. The surrounding region also offers access to nature with lakes, forests and hiking in summer or skiing in winter, so there’s a strong mix of urban and outdoor life.

Non-native English speakers wishing to participate in the English-language curriculum should possess one of the following scores: TOEFL iBT 79, IELTS 6.0, TOEIC 780, CEFR B2, or CET-6 485. Students from Scandinavia, Germany, Austria, The Netherlands, Hong Kong and Singapore are exempt from this requirement, as are students who have an English language education background.

Students intending to take regular university courses instructed in Japanese must possess level N1/level 1 of the Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT), or the equivalent.

column4: Characterized by both tradition and innovation, National Taiwan Normal University (NTNU) has long been recognized as one of Taiwan’s elite institutions of higher education. Founded in 1922, NTNU was formerly an institution dedicated to teacher education. The university later evolved into a comprehensive university that provides students and scholars with opportunities to study and conduct research in a wide range of fields. Throughout its long history, NTNU has always been committed to academic excellence and this is reflected in the many influential educators and researchers that have studied at NTNU over the years.
column18: NTU does not accept exchange students who hold only a Taiwanese passport. Additionally, the College of Medicine requires students to take courses offered by their department. The following courses are not open to exchange students: Practicum courses offered by all departments; Practice courses offered by the Department of Chemistry; All GMBA required/core courses; All EMBA and EIMBA courses; Courses offered by Center for Teaching Education and all Academic Programs (學分學程); School of Professional Education and Continuing Studies (進修推廣學院); Summer Session (暑修) and Summer College (夏季學院) courses. Regarding student status restrictions, please refer to the column "Limits on Course adding/dropping" for each specific course at NTU Online Course Information.

Modules that are open to incoming exchange students are at the undergraduate level in NUS context (level 1000 to 4000). Undergraduate exchange students should consider level 1000 to 3000 modules. Level 4000 modules are mostly reserved for NUS graduating students. Modules of level 5000 and above are graduate modules and are not available to exchange students. Make sure you have read through information about the available and restricted courses at NUS. Computer Science courses are very popular and access to these is extremely limited. Founded in 1831, New York University is one of the largest private universities in the United States. The University, which includes 18 schools, colleges and institutes, occupies five major centers in New York City. There are more than 50,000 students enrolled in all of the schools.

The center of NYU is the Washington Square campus in the heart of Greenwich Village. One of the city's most creative and energetic communities, the Village is a historic neighborhood that has attracted generations of writers, musicians, artists, and intellectuals. Beyond the Village, New York City becomes an extension of the University's campus. In addition to its Manhattan locations, the University is also located in Brooklyn with the second oldest school of engineering and technology in the country, and has numerous research facilities.

column3: Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU)
column4:
The Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU) is Norway’s largest university, with about 43,500 students in total, including a significant number of international students. It has campuses in three cities: Trondheim (its main site), Gjøvik, and Ålesund. NTNU offers a wide variety of programmes across many disciplines: science, technology, engineering, health sciences, humanities, social sciences, etc. Many master’s programmes are taught entirely in English, making it quite accessible for exchange students who don’t want to speak Norwegian.
The location and surroundings of NTNU are a major part of its appeal. Trondheim, where the main campus is located, is a lively student city with a strong blend of history, culture, and nature. You get to enjoy river-side areas, a scenic fjord, forests nearby, islands, and plenty of opportunities for outdoor activities like hiking, skiing, or kayaking. The campuses themselves are fairly well integrated into the city, and public transport is good.

column40:
To be eligible for a KU exchange spot, students must have completed at least one year of study (equivalent to 60 ECTS) by the time they apply for admission to the host university. This must be documented on their transcript by the time of applying to the host university.

First-year bachelor students should note that applications to the host university are submitted before the start of each semester — typically between March and April for the autumn semester and between September and November for the spring semester.

First-year master's students are considered to meet this requirement based on their completed bachelor's degree.
column160:
Pontifical Catholic University of Rio de Janeiro (PUC-Rio) is a private non-profit Catholic university created in 1941. PUC-Rio is a research institution that focuses primarily on the development of science and innovation.
PUC-RIO is located in Gávea, a charming neighbourhood near the forest and beaches of the Zona Sul area of Rio de Janeiro. The university campus is situated within a tropical forest, surrounded by lush trees and with a small stream running through it.

PUC-Rio has more than 12,000 undergraduate students, 2,500 grdauate students and 1,500 faculty members and researchers, and 26 departments organised in four academic centres: Theology and Human Sciences; Social Sciences; Science and Technology; and Biomedical Sciences.
column162:
The agreement is for both undergraduate and graduate studies.
Students from UCPH are also permitted to enroll as internship/research students

Exchange students are able to do internship in Rio de Janeiro while studying at PUC-Rio. Besides being a regular exchange student of the university, it is necessary to endorse the internship at CCCI and at the University`s official Internship Coordination Office (CCESP). For further information, please, visit the university Internship/Research student page.
column164:
Offered twice a year, before the regular academic semester, this 90h intensive course offers the international student the opportunity to learn, develop and experience the Brazilian language and culture before the beginning of the semester so as to be better skilled to attend regular classes.
Four different levels are offered, from very basic to advanced level. Each student takes a written placement test on-line, and an oral one on the first day of classes. Tuition payment required.
More information: https://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/portuguese.html

column4: The university was founded in 1989 and supports a community of over 52,000 students, including around 8,000 from overseas. Queensland University of Technology (QUT) is a major Australian university with a truly global outlook. We’re providing real-world infrastructure, learning and teaching, and graduate skills to the next generation of change-makers. Brisbane is the fastest growing city in Australia and the host of the 2032 Olympics. Our friendly, modern city averages 240 sunny days per year and is the gateway to the stunning and diverse state of Queensland.The QUT International Student Services team organize short trips and weekend activities for international students, so you can see Australia and make friends with international students. See what life is like as an international student at QUT

column4: The history of Ritsumeikan dates back to 1869 when Prince Kinmochi Saionji, an eminent international statesman of modern Japan, founded "Ritsumeikan" as a private academy on the site of the Kyoto Imperial Palace. Today, Ritsumeikan University offers a wide range of courses in advanced studies at its Kinugasa Campus in Kyoto, Biwako‑Kusatsu Campus (BKC) in Shiga and Osaka‑Ibaraki Campus (OIC) in Osaka. The Study in Kyoto Program (SKP) brings together students from around the world. It offers numerous courses in Japanese language and culture, utilizing the extensive historical and cultural resources of the ancient city of Kyoto. The program also offers courses in English regarding both Japan‑related and business subjects. Kyoto, the capital of Japan from 794 to 1868, is an ideal place for study, being a city with one of the highest concentrations of academic institutions in Japan. Kyoto is home to some of Japan's most important World Heritage Sites such as the Ryoanji, Ninnaji and Kinkakuji (Golden Pavilion) Temples, which are located right at the doorstep of Ritsumeikan University. It enjoys a harmonious blend of the ancient and the modern, thus giving students the unique opportunity to experience all aspects of Japanese society. By studying in SKP at Ritsumeikan University, students are able to take advantage of the rich academic and cultural environment of this city, leading to a truly fruitful and memorable study experience in Japan.

Seoul National University honors the ideals of liberal education and aims to teach students a lifelong love of learning that will form the basis for continuous personal growth.

column175:
All applicants must currently be enrolled in at least second semester (= have already completed at least one semester) at SNU's application stage (i.e. before March for exchange in the following Fall Semester, and before September for exchange in the following Spring Semester).

SNU cannot accept graduate exchange students who did not complete at least one semester of their graduate program once SNU's application period starts. In other words, you must have at least one semester’s grades on your graduate transcript at the application stage to SNU. This means that graduate students should consider Seoul National University for exchange only during the third or fourth semester of their graduate programme.

column4: Located on British Columbia's west coast Simon Fraser University opened in 1965 with 2,500 students. The university is now boasting more than 37,000 students and three campuses: Burnaby Campus, Surrey Campus and Vancouver Campus. The Burnaby- and the Surrey Campus are both approximately 40 min from downtown Vancouver, but can be easily reached with the famous Expo Line. SFU is the first Canadian member of the National Collegiate Athletic Association, the world’s largest college sports association.
column12: Fall semester: September-December; Winter semester: January-April; Please note that the spring semester begins very early compared to the spring semester at UCPH. You should only apply for the spring semester, if you do not have exams in January at UCPH.
column18: For students who wants to apply for graduate studies at SFU: The vast majority of exchange students at SFU are admitted as undergraduates. At Simon Fraser University, undergraduate degrees are generally four years in length. This means that many exchange students who are graduate students at their home universities may actually be better suited to take undergraduate Upper-Division courses (300 and 400 level) at SFU, especially if they are in the first year of their graduate degrees. Note that being admitted as a graduate student may impact which SFU Residence building for which you are eligible. Graduate students will only be able to apply for courses from one department, and this is the department to which we will request students to be admitted. While you can request courses from additional departments, access cannot be guaranteed. There are many specialized and cohort-based graduate programs at SFU, which exchange students may not be able to access.

column4: SMU was established on 29 July 2000 and is located in the Downtown area of Singapore. It has a city campus with a total enrolment of about 10,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students and comprises six schools offering undergraduate, graduate and PhD programmes in business administration, business analytics, financial services, accountancy, economics, information systems management, law and the social sciences.   The university has over 30 research institutes and centres of excellence, and customised corporate training and lifelong learning for individuals are available through the university's professional and executive development programmes.  SMU comprises six schools: - School of Accountancy - Lee Kong Chian School of Business - School of Economics - School of Information Systems - School of Law - School of Social Sciences SMU has an emphasis on generating rigorous, high‑impact, and relevant multi‑disciplinary research that addresses Asian issues of global relevance, SMU faculty collaborate with leading international researchers and universities from USA, Europe, China and India as well as with partners in the business community and public sector, through its research institutes, centres and labs. SMU’s city campus is a state‑of‑the‑art facility located in the heart of downtown Singapore, fostering strategic linkages with business, government and the wider community.

column180: Course enrolment is conducted via a bidding exercise in July (Term 1) and December (Term 2) each year. Only undergraduate courses from a prescribed list are applicable to exchange and visiting students (Course Codes 001‑499). Students will be allocated e$100 dollars (one‑time only), for bidding of all their courses. This empowers them to draw up their own timetable based on their recommended study plan (subject to constraints of curriculum requirements and the supply and demand of classes). Details and instructions related to course bidding together with the list of courses offered, shall be made available to students between two to four weeks before the course bidding exercise. It is important that students understand the information, policies and procedures to ensure a smooth bidding process. Demand could be high for some courses, e.g. fundamental freshmen courses. Students are encouraged to be pro‑active and flexible in their study plans to optimise their bidding opportunities.

column4:
Sorbonne University, a prestigious and globally renowned academic institution, stands as a multidisciplinary and research-driven university. Firmly rooted in the heart of Paris, its historical roots trace back to 1257, making it one of the oldest universities in Europe. With its three faculties encompassing Arts & Humanities, Medicine, and Science and Engineering, the university is dedicated to nurturing student excellence and addressing the scientific complexities of the modern era. Serving as a beacon of higher education and research, Sorbonne University holds a distinguished position within the French academic landscape.
Furthermore, Sorbonne is a part of 4EU+ Alliance with the purpose of laying the foundation for an innovative European university model. Sorbonne is fully committed to engage with both its students and partners on an international scale. The university believes that internalization and innovation go hand in hand. In terms of student life, Sorbonne offers more than 80 sporting and artistic activities which are free to join as students off the university.

column15:
For students taking courses at the Faculty of Lettres (Humanities): 
A French language certificate (minimum level B2) is required: Official tests such as DALF, DELF, TCF, Alliance Française, official language level certificate from a teacher or a French language training institute are accepted. OLA certificates are also accepted as proof of French language proficiency, on one condition: It must be accompanied by a letter from a professor confirming the student's language level.
For students taking courses at the Faculty of Science and Engineering:
As an exception: Undergraduate applicants wishing to join the Physics Department must demonstrate a minimum C1 level through a recognized language test such as TCF, DELF or DALF. For other study areas with The Faculty of Science and Engineering no official test is required. However, we recommend a minimum B2 level in either English or French, depending on the language of the selected courses.
Generally, UCPH strongly recommends the higher level C1 (advanced proficiency in French) when studying at Sorbonne University - to make sure that you can cope sufficiently in courses taught in French.
column18:
The Master students can take BA and MA level courses, but the Bachelor students can only take BA level courses. If BA students are interested in a MA level course, they must ask the academic coordinator and the professor of the MA course if they can take this course.
Most courses are taught in French, with the exception of certain language departments where courses are taught in both the learned language and French. It is very unlikely that student can find a full study load in English and must therefore be very proficient in French.
You must be able to get a pre-approval of 30 ECTS from UCPH per semester.
Restrictions for UCPH-students:
- Political Science-students (Statskundskab) at BA-level cannot find courses at Sorbonne for their semester 5
- There are no courses in Economics at Sorbonne
- There are no courses in Law at Sorbonne

column21:
On this exchange agreement you may look for interesting study areas with the two faculties below:
Faculté de Lettres/Faculty of Arts and Humanities - discover the Schools and departments
Course catalogue for Faculty of Arts and Humanities: https://moodle-lettres-24.sorbonne-universite.fr/course/view.php?id=403#section-1. When you have chosen e.g. 'lettres' then press 'Tout dépliér' as it may give you access to additional info.
Incoming Erasmus students must take at least 50% of their workload inside their main department. The rest of their workload can be made up of courses from other departments within this Faculty.
Questions to Faculty of Humanities can be sent to; lettres-erasmus-inri@sorbonne-universite.fr
Faculté des Sciences et IngénieriFaculty of Science & Engineering - se info on study areas.
Course catalogue for Faculty of Science & Engineering: https://sciences.sorbonne-universite.fr/formation-sciences/offre-de-formation and list of English courses at FSI can be found here; https://sciences.sorbonne-universite.fr/en/study/our-programmes
Questions to Faculty of Science and Engineering; sciences-dfipve-mobilite-in@sorbonne-universite.fr
Later on at the nomination process, you must be assigned to a specific department and study area within the specific faculty.
Be aware that Sorbonne has different campuses all over the city, so remember to check the transportation time if you take classes in different campuses the same day.
column22:
Sorbonne University does offer some accommodation for exchange students; however, it is important to note that they do not guarantee accommodation, so international students are recommend to look for private housing when possible. Students receive information about housing when nominated for Erasmus.
Consider Fondation Danoise in Cité Universitaire. Accommodation in general is very expensive in Paris, and difficult to find.

column4: Stellenbosch University is a public research university situated in the town of Stellenbosch, South Africa. Other nearby universities are the University of Cape Town and University of the Western Cape. Stellenbosch University (SU) is home to an academic community of 29 000 students (including 4 000 foreign students from 100 countries) as well as 3 000 permanent staff members (including 1 000 academics) on five campuses. The main campus in the picturesque university town of Stellenbosch in the heart of the Western Cape winelands is quite an attraction for students. Stellenbosch is situated about 50 km from Cape Town and just 30 km from Cape Town International Airport. SU has ten faculties: AgriSciences, Economic and Management Sciences, Medicine and Health Sciences, Engineering, Military Sciences, Arts and Social Sciences, Science, Education, Law and Theology.

column4: Stockholm University, established in 1878 as a college and granted university status in 1960, is a renowned public research university located in Stockholm, Sweden. With its rich history and academic excellence, the university attracts over 33,000 students, making it one of the largest universities in Scandinavia. The university comprises four faculties offering a wide range of academic programs. Situated in the world's first national urban park, the campus blends seamlessly with nature, architecture, and modern art, providing a unique and inspiring learning environment. The main campus, Frescati, is conveniently located near an underground railway station, facilitating easy access for students. Stockholm is spread across islands connected by bridges, offering picturesque views of waterways, parks and historic architecture. It’s an easy city to navigate on foot with a lively cultural scene including museums, concerts, cafes and festivals. The surroundings offer easy access to forests, lakes and the Stockholm Archipelago with thousands of islands just a short boat trip away.

column40: SKKU offers more than 7,000 courses throughout the academic year in every field and level of study. Currently, over 30% of courses are conducted in foreign languages, primarily English. SKKU is actively recruiting renowned foreign professors while providing a series of retraining programs for Korean faculty aimed at enhancing language and lecture skills

column4: Tampere University, established in 2019 through the merger of the University of Tampere and Tampere University of Technology, is a culturally committed higher education institution with a mission to educate visionary individuals who understand and shape the world. Boasting six faculties and an annual student intake of around 20,000, Tampere University stands among the largest and most popular universities in Finland with teaching being exclusively in English. The University of Tampere has two campuses in Tampere: a central campus near the city's railway station and the Kauppi campus, situated alongside the Tampere University Hospital. Join the vibrant community of Tampere University and embark on a transformative educational journey.

column4: 15 academic departments. 42,705 students. 594 professors. 378,6 mio. Euros in third-party research funding. 17 Nobel Prize winners. The Technical University of Munich (TUM) is one of Europe’s top universities, and ever since its founding in 1868, it has been at the forefront of innovation. TUM spans several large sites in Bavaria: Munich, Garching, Weihenstephan and Straubing. These are home to the TUM Schools and Departments and the university's most important research facilities. TUM has concentrated its expertise in life sciences in the TUM School of Life Sciences at the green and modern Weihenstephan campus. It is located on the outskirts of Freising and has evolved from the traditional disciplines of agricultural and life sciences.

English taught courses are offered in the last year of the BA, but advanced BSc students (3rd year) are allowed to attend MSc courses if they meet the prerequisites.

It is possible for exchange students to write their Bachelor or master’s thesis during their stay at the Faculty of Mathematics. You must organize a supervisor yourself, but it is not possible to give a general guarantee, that the supervision of a thesis is possible for exchange students.

Mathematics-Economics students are allowed to follow courses at the Department of Economics but must follow min. 60% of the courses within the Department of Mathematics.

column4: The campus of Tsinghua University is situated in northwest Beijing on the site of the former imperial gardens of the Qing Dynasty, and surrounded by a number of historical sites. Tsinghua University was established in 1911, originally under the name “Tsinghua Xuetang”. Since China opened up to the world in 1978, Tsinghua University has developed at a breathtaking pace into a comprehensive research university. At present, the university has 21 schools and 59 departments with faculties in science, engineering, humanities, law, medicine, history, philosophy, economics, management, education and art.

column3: UiT The Arctic University of Norway
column4: UiT is a large, multi-campus university in Northern Norway, with around 17,000 students, globally recognised as a leading centre for Arctic research. It offers a broad spectrum of programmes across its eight faculties covering most disciplines. While Norwegian is the main language of instruction, many courses especially at the master’s level are taught in English. The city of Tromsø is often described as really special: you’re surrounded by dramatic Arctic scenery, with fjords, mountains, and islands all nearby, and you get phenomena like the Midnight Sun in summer and Northern Lights in winter. Tromsø is reasonably walkable, especially in the city centre with decent public transit. Importantly, the setting encourages a lot of outdoor activity, both in winter and summer, so if you enjoy nature, you’ll have plenty of options for hiking, skiing, boating, and exploring both urban and wild landscapes.

column4: The Umeå University is one of Sweden’s leading comprehensive universities, with over 40 000 students enrolled. It offers a large number of programmes and courses taught in English, making it an attractive option for exchange students. The university is known for its strong research and for fostering an open and collaborative academic environment. The city of Umeå provides a friendly and accessible setting for student life, with a compact and walkable central area where cafés, shops and cultural venues are easily reachable from campus. Beyond the urban environment you’ll find vast natural surroundings: forests, rivers, hiking and bike paths and plenty of snow in winter.

column4: The Federal University of Rio de Janeiro – UFRJ has three campi: Cidade Universitária, Praia Vermellha and Macaé, comprising institutes, schools, faculties and supplementary units, which include seven museums, among which we can highlight the National Museum, eight university hospitals, and the third largest ocean basin in the world for research on offshore oil exploration. The university has partnerships with several national and multinational enterprises, some of which have facilities on the very UFRJ campi. It is the case of Petrobras, whose biggest research center is located on Cidade Universitária campus. Following a global trend, the UFRJ establishes and maintains cooperative relationships with institutions from all continents, which now amounts to over 200 international institutions all over the world.

column40: To be eligible for a KU exchange spot, students must have completed at least one year of study (equivalent to 60 ECTS) by the time they apply for admission to the host university. This must be documented on their transcript by the time of applying to the host university. First-year bachelor students should note that applications to the host university are submitted before the start of each semester — typically between March and April for the autumn semester and between September and November for the spring semester. First-year master's students are considered to meet this requirement based on their completed bachelor's degree.

column4: University College Cork (UCC) is one of Ireland’s leading universities, known for its strong academic reputation and commitment to research and innovation. It serves a diverse student body of over 24,000 and offers a wide range of disciplines across its faculties. UCC places a strong emphasis on sustainability, international collaboration, and student-centered learning. The university integrates research with teaching and encourages students to engage with real-world challenges through interdisciplinary approaches and community partnerships. Its campus combines historic architecture with modern facilities, creating a balanced environment for academic and personal development. Cork, located in the south of Ireland, is a lively and welcoming city with a rich cultural heritage and a strong student presence. The city is compact and easy to navigate, with good public transport and a wide range of cafés, music venues, and cultural events. Students can explore nearby coastal areas, historic sites, and scenic countryside, all within easy reach. A fun fact: Cork is home to one of the world’s largest natural harbours and also produces a significant share of the world’s Tic Tacs.
column10: University College Cork - Erasmus - Biological and related science (Science-wide)

column18: You should have completed 2 years (120 ECTS) before completing your exchange at UCC. Some courses may demand particular background knowledge, and you are advised to confirm subject choices with the International Office and/or the College. Students are permitted to choose modules across faculties and years. If BSc students have a sufficient background, they may be able to take a course at the MSc level; however, this depends on the department. The majority of courses available are at the bachelor level. Students can do research projects, but approval will depend on whether students require a supervisor and if so, whether a staff member is available to do so.

column4: Established in 1874, the University of Adelaide is Australia's third oldest university. Adelaide has over 27,000 registered students, around 9,000 of whom are international. By every measure, the University of Adelaide—a member of Australia’s prestigious Group of Eight research-intensive universities—stands tall among the world’s leading institutions of learning and innovation. Considered one of the top 1% of Universities worldwide, we count among our distinguished alumni: five Nobel Laureates; over 100 Rhodes Scholars, including Australia’s first Indigenous recipient; and Australia’s first female prime minister and Supreme Court judge. We occupy a unique position among Australian cities, Adelaide is friendly and offers a real sense of community. Its size and sense of comfort means it provides an atmosphere conducive to study. You want beaches? Festivals? Proximity to beautiful Australian nature and the Outback? Adelaide has all of this - and more!

column4: The University of Agder is a modern, dynamic university in southern Norway with about 14,000 students, spread mainly over two campuses in Kristiansand and Grimstad. While many of the undergraduate courses are taught in Norwegian, there is a solid selection of Master’s‐level and exchange courses in English. The Grimstad campus is especially noted for its engineering, technology, and applied sciences orientation, with modern facilities, while Kristiansand is larger, more central, and offers greater diversity in programme choice. The cities where UiA is based Kristiansand and Grimstad combine the charms of Southern Norway’s coastal landscape with good student amenities and fairly easy living. Kristiansand is the larger city, with a compact centre, decent public transit, green areas, and recreational options close by: forests, beaches, hiking, and water sports. Many services, cafés, shops, and cultural venues are within walking distance if you live in or near the city centre or close to campus, which makes day-to-day life convenient. Grimstad is smaller and the distance from campus to town is short.

column4: Akureyri, a city with significant influence on the development of Northern Iceland, has emerged as a vibrant center of learning. Despite its remote location, trade and export, particularly of seafood products, have facilitated connections with the outside world. With a population of around 17,000, Akureyri is the largest community outside the Reykjavik area. The establishment of the University of Akureyri in 1987 solidified the city's reputation as an educational hub. The University of Akureyri, home to approximately 2,000 students, boasts two campuses. Alongside academic facilities, the university campus offers a cafeteria, a wellness and exercise center, and a 24/7 accessible library. Embrace the enriching educational opportunities and the lively atmosphere of Akureyri as you embark on your academic journey at the University of Akureyri.

column4:
The University of Amsterdam (UvA) is one of Europe’s top research universities, known for its academic excellence, broad disciplinary offerings, and strong international orientation. Founded in 1632, UvA offers a wide range of programs across seven faculties, including humanities, social and behavioral sciences, economics and business, law, and science. With over 40,000 students, including a large international cohort, the university fosters a vibrant and diverse academic community. UvA is particularly recognized for its research-led teaching, interdisciplinary collaboration, and commitment to addressing global challenges through innovative scholarship.
Located in the heart of Amsterdam, the university offers students the opportunity to study in one of the most culturally rich and globally connected cities in Europe. Amsterdam is known for its historic canals, world-class museums, and dynamic urban life. The city’s compact layout, excellent public transport, and welcoming atmosphere make it ideal for student living. Whether exploring the city’s artistic heritage, engaging in public debates, or enjoying its lively cafés and green spaces, exchange students at UvA will find themselves in an inspiring environment that supports both academic growth and personal discovery.

Exchange students nominated to the Faculty of Science are expected to take their courses at the Faculty of Science. Exchange students can apply for a maximum of two courses at another department or faculty. Please note that placement at other departments is difficult and cannot be guaranteed.
BSc-students are not allowed to follow master courses. It is therefore recommended that you follow one of the Minors offered at the Faculty of Science. These are pre-packaged selections of related courses that fulfill the 30 ECTS requirement.
There are fewer English taught courses offered in semester 2. If there is not a full semester load of courses offered in your own study field, you can choose additional Interdisciplinary courses or courses in Scientific Programming, both at bachelor level.
Erasmus students who want to take courses at the UvA have to be in Amsterdam when the courses start. You will not be allowed to take a course if you have missed the first class(es).

column4: Founded in 1883, Auckland is the country’s largest university with over 40,000 students, nearly 10,000 of whom graduate annually. The main campus is in the heart of Auckland city and we have four specialist campuses. The University of Auckland is New Zealand’s world-ranked university. The University of Auckland is the only New Zealand university ranked in the top 200 in the Times Higher Education World University Rankings, in the top 100 in the QS World University Rankings, and with the maximum rating of Five Stars Plus in the QS Stars University Ratings. The University is also the highest ranked New Zealand university in the Shanghai Jiao Tong Academic Ranking of World Universities. Auckland’s harbour setting, magnificent beaches and verdant bush make it a great place to study and work, while the University’s close proximity to New Zealand’s major business city enables staff and students to rub shoulders with leading practitioners in their field. For example, medical students work alongside leading specialists at Auckland City Hospital while the Faculty of Law is within walking distance of the courts and major legal firms. The Business School has a close relationship with leading Auckland companies and academics from our Faculty of Engineering, School of Population Health and School of Architecture and Planning serve on think-tanks helping to shape the city’s future.

column4: The University of Barcelona, founded in 1450, is one of Spain’s largest and most prestigious public universities. It serves a student population of over 60,000 and offers a comprehensive range of academic disciplines across its faculties. The university is recognized for its strong research output, international collaborations, and commitment to academic excellence. Its campuses are integrated into the city, combining historic buildings with modern facilities and providing students with access to extensive libraries, research centers, and support services. Barcelona offers a dynamic setting for student life, with a Mediterranean climate, rich cultural heritage, and efficient public transport. The city is known for its architecture, beaches, and vibrant neighborhoods, making it easy for students to balance academic pursuits with leisure and cultural activities. A fun fact: Barcelona’s main campus is located near the Eixample district, famous for its grid layout and iconic works by architect Antoni Gaudí, including the Sagrada Família and Casa Batlló.

column4: The University of Bergen is a public research university located on Norway’s west coast. Established in 1946, it is one of the country’s leading academic institutions, serving around 20,000 students across six faculties. The university offers a wide range of study programmes and is known for its strong focus on interdisciplinary research, particularly in marine sciences, climate studies, and global health. Bergen, Norway’s second-largest city, offers a unique setting for student life, surrounded by mountains and fjords, making it an ideal place for students who enjoy outdoor activities alongside their studies. The city is compact and walkable, with reliable public transport. Bergen is also known for its rich cultural heritage, vibrant music scene, and historic waterfront district, Bryggen, which is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

The university has been welcoming international students onto its campus since its inception in 1900 – and with over 5,000 international students from over 150 different countries, it has one of the largest and most vibrant international student communities in the UK.

University of Copenhagen MA-students considering taking undergraduate modules at the Univeristy of Birmingham must make sure that they can get approval from their faculty to transfer undergraduate (bachelor) modules from the host university into their programmes here at UCPH. column4: The University of Bristol has had a reputation for innovation since its founding in 1876. The diversity of a centre for cutting‑edge art, music and culture, coupled with being a historical city surrounded by countryside, make studying at Bristol a rich and exciting experience. Right in the heart of the city are many University buildings and facilities.

column4: University of British Columbia was founded in 1908 and supports a community of almost 60,000 students whereof 12,000 are international students. UBC is a two-campus university: Okanagan campus and Vancouver campus. Vancouver Campus is located 30 minutes from the heart of downtown Vancouver in a spectacular ‘must-see’ location. The Okanagan campus is nestled in the middle of the stunning Okanagan Valley and is known for its small class sizes and first-name terms with the professors.

You cannot apply to the individual UC campuses in your application for exchange. Students apply for admission to 3 UC campuses after nomination for exchange.

column18:
This agreement is for undergraduate studies only. Students who have passed 240 ECTS or more at university level by the time of departure cannot apply for this agreement.
You have to apply for a minimum of two quarters or one semester. If you apply for fall, your exchange period will last from September to March.
Spring semester from January to June.
There are 9 UC campuses in total. After your nomination from UCPH, you will start your application to UC. You must apply to at least three different campuses, and the University of California Education Abroad programme (UCEAP) will decide which campus you are placed at.
University of California health insurance is mandatory.
Incoming students can only apply for exchange for a maximum of 1 academic year in total due to UCEAP restrictions. At UC, this is either 3 quarters or 2 semesters.
This means, that if you have already been on exchange at UC from KU, you will not fulfill the requirements for attending UC for 2 quarters or 1 semester, as they cannot send you to the same campus again, nor guarantee that your application for a specific campus will be successful.
You cannot apply for a second time.

column25:
Berkeley & Merced: 13 units (semester units) = 30 ECTS

Davis, Irvine, Los Angeles, Riverside, San Diego, Santa Barbara & Santa Cruz (quarter campuses): 18 units (quarter units) = 30 ECTS

Immigration requirement: To meet the immigration requirements, all students at the above mentioned quarter campuses must enroll in 12 units per quarter. As the duration of an exchange stay is at least two quarters, students must enroll in courses corresponding to 24 units, which is more than the 18 units required to obtain 30 ECTS. Transfer of excess units to ECTS will be handled according to the rules of the relevant UCPH faculty (due to the block structure at the Faculty of Science at UCPH, students from this faculty may consider taking 27 units, as this would give them the opportunity to transfer 45 ECTS before they return for block 4).

column4:
Established in 1873 by scholars from Oxford and Cambridge, the University of Canterbury, in Christchurch, New Zealand, has an international reputation for academic excellence in both teaching and research. Teaching staff are actively involved in research and publishing in their areas of speciality. As a result, even undergraduate students can expect to be taught by lecturers who are at the forefront of knowledge in their field. The University’s world‐class education is also enhanced by the many outstanding international academics who visit each year on our staff exchange programmes. Students may be taught by academic staff from the universities of Oxford or Cambridge, among many others.

The University is situated in a spacious landscaped campus in the suburb of Ilam, Christchurch, only 15 minutes from the central city and 10 minutes from the International Airport. The campus is attractively landscaped, with plenty of green space to relax with friends between lectures. Modern facilities include four libraries, computer suites with 24‐ hour access, lecture theatres, laboratories, studios, six halls of residence and a host of student services and facilities, including cafés, bars, a health centre and pharmacy, recreation facilities, childcare facilities, shops, an art gallery and an events centre.

column4:
Situated between the Alps and the Jura mountains, at the southwestern tip of Switzerland and Lake Geneva, Geneva is in the heart of Western Europe. Its ideal geographical location has made the city a prosperous hub for trade and exchanges of all kinds. This cosmopolitan city is home to more than 180 nationalities and is recognized worldwide for its spirit of openness and tolerance.
Considered the smallest of the major world capitals, Geneva, which is home to some thirty international organizations including the UN, WTO, WHO and ILO and more than 300 NGOs and permanent missions of foreign states, has a true international vocation.This multicultural environment is reflected at the University of Geneva, where about one third of the students are foreign nationals. The university pursues a very active policy of international relations; in particular, it participates in numerous bi- or multilateral agreements, networks and programs such as Erasmus and Socrates.
Ideally located near the most famous ski areas of the Alps, not far from the Mediterranean, while being anchored in Central Europe, Geneva offers the rare advantages of a cosmopolitan city on a human scale. Cuisine and music from all over the world are found here. Lovers of green spaces will find an atmosphere conducive to meditation in its parks or on the shores of Lake Geneva. Culture lovers will be spoilt for choice, thanks to the presence in Geneva of one of the most prestigious opera scenes in Europe, several film festivals, and numerous concert halls and theaters.
The University of Geneva takes an active part in this cultural scene. Its film club and choir in particular attract large audiences. But Geneva also lives to the rhythm of a multitude of associations, representing the most diverse countries and interests, which make it uniquely suited for the exchange of ideas and traditions.

column4: The University of Gothenburg, founded in 1891, is one of Sweden’s largest and most respected universities, with around 58,000 students. It offers a broad academic portfolio across eight faculties with plenty of elements available in English. The university is renowned for its high-quality research often ranking among the top 200 universities worldwide. The university does not sit on one single sprawling campus; instead its faculties and departments are spread across a number of major locations within the city of Gothenburg Living in Gothenburg adds to the appeal. Sweden’s second-largest city, with about 600,000 residents, combines a lively urban environment with the relaxed feel of a coastal town. Students enjoy the city’s canals, green spaces, vibrant music and food scenes, and excellent public transport that makes exploring easy. Meanwhile, there are plenty of outdoor possibilities, including quick escapes to the nearby archipelago.

The University of Helsinki is Finland’s largest and oldest university, with about 31,000 students across four campuses. It offers a wide range of English-taught courses and programmes, making it a welcoming choice for exchange students. While Finnish and Swedish are the official languages, English is widely used in both academic and social settings, ensuring that international students can easily participate in studies and campus life.
Helsinki itself is a safe, student-friendly city surrounded by nature and the sea. Its compact size makes it easy to get around, and students can enjoy both lively urban culture and peaceful outdoor areas within minutes. From waterfront cafés and island trips to forests and parks near the campuses,

column4:
The University of Hong Kong (HKU) is the oldest university in Hong Kong. As an Englishmedium university with a history that stretches back more than 100 years, it has grown with and helped shape the city from which it takes its name.
Today, HKU is recognized internationally as a dynamic and comprehensive university of world-class standing. With its distinguished excellence in research and outstanding performance in teaching, it attracts first-class teaching and research staff and brilliant students from around the world.
The University’s research students enjoy opportunities to work with leading international researchers and a peer group of resourceful and committed students from different nations in advanced facilities, producing cutting-edge and internationally recognized research work, and developing skills essential for future careers and lifelong learning.
In 2003, during the outbreak of Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome (SARS), HKU medical researchers became the first in the world to identify the coronavirus that caused the SARS.

column4: The University of Iceland is the country’s oldest and largest university, with roughly 14,000 students enrolled across undergraduate, graduate, and PhD programmes. It comprises five schools and over 25 faculties, offering more than 400 programmes in many fields including sciences, humanities, health and social sciences. Classes are mainly taught in Icelandic, but there is a growing number of English-taught courses, especially at Master’s level. Reykjavík offers a vibrant cultural life, easy access to nature, and a welcoming atmosphere. You’ll be close to museums, cafés and arts events, while also having quick access to outstanding natural beauty: volcanic landscapes, geothermal pools, glaciers, waterfalls and long summer days (or northern lights in winter).

column4: The University of Jyväskylä is a dynamic and esteemed multidisciplinary institution in Finland. With six faculties and independent institutes, it stands as one of the largest and most popular universities in the country. Founded in 1863 as the first Finnish-speaking teacher training college, Jyväskylä Teacher Seminary, it has evolved into a nationally and internationally recognized research university. Known for its expertise in education, human sciences, and natural sciences, the University of Jyväskylä attracts top-tier students and is highly selective. Embrace a transformative academic journey at this prestigious institution, ranked as the second largest university in Finland.

In addition, most courses require writing essays and research papers. This means that applicants should be at least B1 but preferably B2 (or higher) level users of the English language. These requirements do not apply to students whose Finnish skills are good enough to carry out their studies in Finnish.

column160:
Kent Law School is one of the UK’s leading law schools. We have a distinctive critical approach, placing law within the wider context of society. Recognised excellence in teaching, world-class research, and an award-winning law clinic create an intellectually stimulating environment in which to study the law.

Kent Law School is based on the Canterbury campus, which is set in 300 acres of parkland. Everything you need is within walking distance. You can watch a play or a film at the Gulbenkian arts centre, dance at The Venue nightclub, keep fit at our sports centre and meet friends at one of many campus cafés and restaurants.

The campus is a 25-minute walk from Canterbury, a lovely city with a world-famous cathedral, medieval buildings, lively pubs and restaurants and a wide range of shops. There’s a vibrant cultural scene, including an annual arts festival, and the coast and countryside are within easy reach. London is less than an hour away by high-speed train.

column4: Discover the University of Lapland, a renowned international and multidisciplinary university located in Rovaniemi, on the Arctic Circle. With expertise in art and design, education, law, social sciences, and Northern and Arctic issues, as well as tourism research, this university offers a diverse range of academic disciplines. For the Faculty of Law, the University of Lapland is a key player in legal education, responsible for educating a significant portion of Finland's lawyers. Their innovative degree structure combines various modes of study, fostering a balance between practical skills and theoretical knowledge. Within the faculty, three prominent institutes specialize in Air and Space Law, Commercial Law, and Law and Informatics. Additionally, the faculty provides a comprehensive selection of English-taught courses, ensuring accessibility for international students.

column21: Students should complete the module enrolment form sent in their acceptance email with their preferred module codes. The Teaching Schools will then enrol them on the modules.
